rulings.Case Title: TODD MARINCHAK VS. SPARKS FAMILY TRUST DATED JULY 5,2004 A TRUST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Source Surgical And Todd Marinchak'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 16, 2009, line 9, Plaintiffs Source Surgical and Todd Marinchaks's Order to Show Cause regarding preliminary injunction. Plaintiff's Motion to Stay Dissolution is GRANTED, Plaintiff IS ENTITLED TO THE REQUESTED RELIEF UNDER CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 2000(b). Plaintiff¿s OSC re Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANT SPARKS. COURT ISSUES AN INJUNCTION AGAINST DEFENDANT SPARKS THAT MIRRORS THE RESTRAINING ORDER ENTERED ON 2/23/09. SPARKS IS ALSO ORDERED TO RETURN ALL OF SOURCE SURGICAL¿S PROPERTY, INCLUDING THE COMPUTER BACK UP TAPES. PLAINTIFF TO POST A $750,000 BOND PER CCP 529. DENY AS TO DEFENDANTS AARSHEIM AND WOMACK. PLAINTIFF IS UNLIKELY TO PREVAIL AGAINT THEM ON ITS MISAPPROPRIATION CLAIM BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH THE EXISTENCE OF A TRADE SECRET. See American Paper & Packaging Products, Inc. v. Kirgan, (1986) 183 Cal.App.3d 1318, 1326. PLAINTIFF IS UNLIKELY TO PREVAIL ON ITS BREACH OF TRADE SECRETS CONTRACT CLAIM BECAUSE IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER WOMACK EXECUTED THAT AGREEMENT. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF¿S REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AGAINST AARSHEIM AND WOMACK VIOLATES BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 16600 AS AN ILLEGAL RESTRAINT OF TRADE. =(302/CWW/PB)
